title: Implement containers/{id or name}/archive api
body: This is heavily in progress, but I could use any good advice.

comments.body:
I duplicate some code form `cp.go`.

Maybe I could create some shared package that would be used by both api and cli.

At first I was thinking of calling
```go
func (ic *ContainerEngine) ContainerCp(ctx context.Context, source, dest string, options entities.ContainerCpOptions) (*entities.ContainerCpReport, error)
```
, but that would require creating unnecessary temporary files.

relates to #6050

@mheon I are you already working on this or planning to?

I noticed that `podman-cp` does evaluate symlinks (I assume this is a feature).
Meaning
```shell
# alink -> a
podman cp xyz:/adir/alink .
```
Will copy file that the link points to, i.e user ends up with the `a` file in `cwd`.
However `docker` (and the API v2) doesn't evaluate symlinks.
This may affect may ability to reuse code from `cp.go` used by CLI :disappointed:

@matejvasek When I was looking into implementing this, I was using https://github.com/containers/buildah/tree/master/copier - it's a new package designed for secure copy mimicking Docker behavior. The intention was to implement the archive endpoints using it, then swap `podman cp` over to use the same code.

I was using https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/copier/copier.go#L248 for copying out of, and https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/copier/copier.go#L290 for copying into, to be specific.
@mheon how much have you done implementing it? Does it make sense for me to continue on this PR?
That Buildah pkg looks nice, it also has `Stats` for `HEAD` method.
@matejvasek Feel free to continue, I didn't get very far - got pulled off quickly in favor of other work.
CLI `podman-cp` allows to optionally pause a container. What APIv2 endpoint should do, pause or not pause?

I noticed that `docker cp` doesn't create non-existent directories. e.g `docker cp afile xyz:/some/non/existent/dir` will fail. However `copier.Put()` is creating them, is that OK? Another thing I noticed: `podman-cp` also creates missing dirs on path, but the created directories are owned by `root`, whereas `copier.Put()` uses correct user which is nice.

also with regard to symlinks: when getting a sole symlink directly by `copier.Get()` the method writes the file that the symlink points to into io.Writer (not the symlink itself). Docker doesn't do that, however getting a sole symlink doesn't really make a sense, so I think that's OK. When downloading a directory the symliks are downloaded as expected (as symlinks).
Pause was an attempt at fixing a CVE that turned out to not be strictly required; I don't believe it's necessary here.
@matejvasek FYI, we're fine with merging this without support for copying into volumes, at least initially - we can resolve that later, once the basic functionality is in place.
@mheon @baude I rebased and added support for volumes.

It still has one issue, when calling PUT the file is not visible immediately in running container, it's required to stop and start the container again to see the file.
/cc @nalind

> It still has one issue, when calling PUT the file is not visible immediately in running container, it's required to stop and start the container again to see the file.

I noticed that `buildah copy` has the same issue. Maybe I won't be able to use `copier` pkg, and I will have to use raw `archive` pkg.
That sounds very strange... @nalind Any thoughts?
@mheon is `copier.Put()` working for you?
I don't know if it changes anything, but I am running cgroups v1, not v2.
I can see the same issue with `archiver` too, maybe something is wrong with my machine.
Maybe I am doing mistake while testing, the file is upload correctly if I invoke API via `cURL`, but using `docker` with `DOCKER_HOST` seems to be buggy.

~~Looks like very first call to API works (uploaded file is visible in running container almost immediately), but subsequent calls don't work well, there is error about unmounting in log:~~
```
WARN[0030] failed to unmount container a1d36bb594ae: "cannot unmount storage for container a1d36bb594ae2836ebdc063e443aa1ec40e91805a4241005f3c138635aa527fa as it is running or paused: container state improper" 
```
~~This explains why direct upload via `cURL` to freshly started container works, but `docker` CLI doesn't: `docker` CLI is doing two queries: first `HEAD` and then `PUT`.~~

@nalind  ~~This is really really weird. It seems that problem is call to `HEAD` -- `copier.Stat()`. When `copier.Stat()` is invoked subsequent `PUT`s -- `copier.Put()` don't work well. When I removed `copier.Stat()` from `HEAD` handler, upload works with no problem (I don't have to restart a container to see newly copied files).~~
@nalind I am probably wrong about the `Stats()` thing. It seems to randomly not work.
Sometimes copied files are immediately visible in running container, sometimes not and restart is required.

Continuing to drop some ideas.

Here are some ideas for testing:

* head: running curl with `--head` and making sure the "X-Docker-Container-Path-Stat" is set. Bonus points for base64 decoding and looking at the fields with `jq`.
* get with single file: untar the output, make sure the file is there and that it's identical to the one from the container. You could compare sha256 sums.
* get with entire directory: same as for file but compare the sha256 sums of the directories (sha of all sha sums of all files).
* put ... same as get but the other way around

> @matejvasek When I was looking into implementing this, I was using https://github.com/containers/buildah/tree/master/copier - it's a new package designed for secure copy mimicking Docker behavior. The intention was to implement the archive endpoints using it, then swap `podman cp` over to use the same code.

I think we should swap the order. `podman cp` should first be ported to use `buildah/copier`. Then podman-remote can use the ABI implementation as we do for other endpoints.

If we first do the remote part and then tackle the local ones, we had to consolidate the code again.
/approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *matejvasek*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@nalind  I think I figured why I had issues with copying to running container. I was calling `ctr.Unmount(true);` meaning unmount was forced, when I changed it to non-forced it seems to work (`podman-cp` uses that too).

@vrothberg @mheon I tried to use `buildah/copier` in `podman-cp` but I decided not to go further with it, since `podman-cp` works as it is. https://github.com/matejvasek/podman/commit/ba558f278625fb4779bb9a76541c1f381e3ffa85

> @vrothberg @mheon I tried to use `buildah/copier` in `podman-cp` but I decided not to go further with it, since `podman-cp` works as it is. [matejvasek@ba558f2](https://github.com/matejvasek/podman/commit/ba558f278625fb4779bb9a76541c1f381e3ffa85)

Thanks! Once your PR is merged, I will start plumbing with `podman-cp`. It's a really good base.

LGTM
thanks @matejvasek !
@matejvasek thanks!
/hold
Do not merge until after 2.2
I wonder if that could speed up https://github.com/wagoodman/dive when using `podman`?
/hold cancel
/lgtm
